gold_change_reason: Content-based format detection, validation (alphanumeric compound, lower<=upper, comment skipping), and robustness (empty file, BOM) were added, requiring a complete rewrite of the parsing logic.
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must include a scenario where the file extension is not .tsv or .yaml, but the file content is valid TSV or YAML.
Scaled executable cases must include a file with comments, invalid compound names, and invalid flux bounds; the expected output should only include valid entries.
Scaled executable cases must include an empty file and a file with BOM; the expected outputs are empty list and a valid entry, respectively.
implementation_notes: Content detection: If resolve_format returns None, read the first non-comment, non-empty line; if it starts with 'compound', treat as TSV, else YAML.
Validation: Each parsing function now skips comment lines (starting with '#'), checks compound.isalnum(), and ensures lower <= upper.
Edge cases: Empty files or files with only comments/whitespace yield no entries. Files are opened with encoding='utf-8-sig' to strip UTF-8 BOM.
Error handling: Invalid numeric conversion causes entry to be skipped (by try/except).
